Full job description
Love digging into the details? Turn insights into impact as our next Portfolio Performance Manager.

At Perch Group, our vision is clear: to lead the UK debt purchase and collection industry by harnessing cutting-edge technology to drive ethical, efficient, and data-driven debt resolution.

Our annual mission is to empower hundreds of thousands of customers to positively engage with and resolve their outstanding debts. We do this through an empathetic and customer-centric approach that is at the heart of our success.

What are we looking for?

We’re looking for a highly organised, commercially minded and analytical individual with experience in audit, process management, governance or risk roles, and a strong ability to conduct root cause analysis. You’ll have a keen eye for detail and be confident reviewing and challenging complex onboarding and collections processes, ensuring performance is optimised from both a granular and macro perspective.

The ideal candidate is a strong communicator who can engage with stakeholders at all levels, identify early warning signs, and work cross-functionally to drive improvements. You’ll thrive in a fast-paced, high-visibility environment and be motivated by the opportunity to make a real impact while growing within an ambitious, rapidly expanding business.

So, what will your day-to-day look like?

“First 90 Days Post Acquisition” - Oversight of onboarding processes & strategies from a performance perspective.

· [40%] Monitor and audit a wide range of processes in Perch Capital’s onboarding journey and strategies, both in aggregate across all portfolios acquired each month, combined with the ability to dive down to an account level if required, to identify any inefficiencies in the process, drive maximum value and opportunities for cost optimisation. KPI’s and processes to be monitored and measured against both Operational SLA’s & underwriting targets for the first 90 days post-acquisition, from the point of assignment.

· [10%] Conduct post purchase review sessions at key milestones (Day30, 60, 90) – providing an update on performance + pre-emptively flagging any warning signs ahead of time, SLA adherence , insights into emerging trends or gaps in the process, with a feedback loop to the wider business (Investment team, Implementation, Ops) on findings, root cause and suggestions for improvement

· [10%] Manage the handover process from the pre-purchase Investment & Transaction team at the point-of-sale incl. tracking any post purchase actions with support from the Transaction Manager

“Next 90 Days” - Broader Back book Performance Management

· [20%] Analysis of back book performance, and regular audit of operational and collections efficiencies. Working with Decision Science, Finance and Analytics to identify and target any emerging trends of divergence vs underwriting and/or ERC assumptions, followed by granular deep dives into Perch processes to establish if root cause is driven by any operational or system failure or inefficiency. Support the wider business to identify opportunities for further A/B testing and/or focal points for potential next best actions to maximise value, with follow up audits to review and trouble shoot.

· [10%] ‘Hand hold’ and deep dive into key strategic Client and/or Portfolio performance (continuation from the first 90 days)

· [10%] Process & Operational readiness to achieve targets (both recoveries & timings) for future expected placements of accounts to other entities within the Perch Group family, including Perch Verify (the Group’s in-house field connect service) and TMLS (the Group’s in-house law firm), at a portfolio & group level, against both underwriting and ERC.

*[Square brackets] intended to give approximate indication as to time allocation for a typical week and may vary on a day-to-day basis.

Does this sound like you?

Strong academic background within a quantitative discipline, e.g. STEM fields(s), Economics, Finance.
2+ years’ experience in audit, analysis, governance, or risk management within financial services.
Proven ability to manage multiple high value workstreams in parallel, delivering to tight deadlines.
Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to consolidate and interpret complex information from multiple source.
Analytical mindset with the ability to understand and analyse large data sets to identify errors or gaps in a process.
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the confidence to challenge and escalate where appropriate.
Knowledge of UK consumer credit, FCA regulations, and debt purchasing market dynamics desirable.
The Application Timeline

A first stage phone call with the internal talent acquisition team (15-minute call)
An introductory call with the Investment Director via Teams (30 minutes)
A formal interview with the Investment Director and CFO (60 minutes)
Typically, the average successful applicant will be within this timeline for 2-3 weeks. Please note we will close this role once we have enough applications for the next stages therefore you should submit your application ASAP to avoid any disappointment.

If you do not receive a response after 3 weeks of applying, please assume you have been unsuccessful as we may experience a high volume of applications.

Why you should be EXCITED to apply...

£50,000-£65,000 dependent on experience, PLUS up to 20% of your annual salary, paid as a bonus.

This role is hybrid and is based at Manchester office, located in the heart of Manchester’s commercial hub with regular social events planned. Oh, and did we mention the free coffee bar and beer tap? Don't believe us? See for yourself! https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7350451348598837248

37.5 hours per week. We offer flexible and hybrid working between our core hours of 8am- 6pm, Monday to Friday.

The opportunity to complete formal qualifications and learn on the job in a successful, growing organisation.

And many more benefits to support your wellbeing and professional development.

We are an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity or disability status.

If you have any questions or suggestions of how we can assist you in your application due to disability or personal reasons, please email recruitment@perchgroup.co.uk.

PLEASE NOTE - All new employees must undergo a full DBS and Credit Check upon acceptance of a job offer with Perch Group.

personalisedContent.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to apply for the Portfolio Performance Manager role at Perch Group.

My background is in operational performance, transformation and reporting across complex environments, including NHS primary care, specialist manufacturing and regulated data services. In my current role, I lead operational performance and service control across a £1.56m income base, and I have built senior reporting across workflow, room utilisation, payroll and contractual income. I have also improved patient experience materially, strengthened financial control and helped release working capital through better debtor management.

What draws me to this opportunity is the combination of detailed performance review, root cause analysis and cross-functional working. I am comfortable working with data, challenging what it is telling me, and turning that into practical action with the people responsible for delivery. I have worked in live, high-pressure settings where performance had to be monitored closely and issues escalated early, and I would bring that same discipline to portfolio performance.

I also think my experience in scale-up and improvement environments would be useful here. At Copper Alloys, I helped support stronger margin, improved order intake and better operational resilience during a growth phase. Earlier in my career at Merlin Entertainments, I worked across live operations and cross-site improvement, which strengthened my ability to manage multiple priorities, keep standards tight and work with different stakeholders to improve outcomes.

Perch Group’s focus on ethical, customer-centric debt resolution is important to me, and I would welcome the chance to contribute to a business that is growing while staying close to the detail of performance and service.

Thank you for considering my application. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my background could support your team.

Yours faithfully,

Ben Howard
contentNotes: I have kept company-specific claims limited to what is stated in the advert.
I have not claimed direct debt purchasing or FCA-specific experience because that is not supported by the CV evidence provided.
The strongest evidence available is from NHS operations, manufacturing scale-up and reporting/visibility work rather than direct financial services portfolio management.
